title: Mass Invariance of the Pairwise Hamiltonian Lie Algebra

theorem.statement: Let H_ij = p_i^2/(2m_i) + p_j^2/(2m_j) - m_i*m_j*u_ij be the pairwise Hamiltonian for the 1/r potential in d spatial dimensions, where m_i are arbitrary nonzero masses treated as elements of the rational function field Q(m_1,...,m_N). Let L_k denote the cumulative dimension (rank over Q(m_1,...,m_N)) of the Lie algebra generated by iterated Poisson brackets of {H_ij} through bracket level k. Then L_k is independent of the masses m_i.
evidence: Symbolic computation: rank over Q(m_1,m_2,m_3) gives [3, 6, 17, 116] through level 3 (N=3, d=2)
theorem.proof_sketch: The computation over Q(m_1,m_2,m_3) is the definitive one. Since the rank of a matrix over a field is a semi-continuous function (it can only drop at special values), the rank computed over the function field Q(m_1,...,m_N) is the GENERIC rank — it holds for all but a measure-zero set of mass values. Since we additionally verified the same rank at multiple specific mass values (including extreme ratios like 1:1:1/10000), and the rank never drops, the mass invariance holds everywhere on the open dense set of positive masses.
theorem.scope: Verified for N=3, d=2, 1/r potential through level 3. The same argument applies in principle to any N and d, but the symbolic computation has only been completed for N=3.
theorem.implication: The algebraic structure of the pairwise Hamiltonian Lie algebra is determined entirely by the potential type, the number of bodies N, and the spatial dimension d. The masses play no role in the algebra's dimension. This means the algebraic obstruction to integrability (if any) is universal across all mass ratios.
theorem.numerical_caveat: Numerical atlases using SVD show apparent rank drops at extreme mass ratios (e.g., Sun-Earth-Moon gives 102-108, Sun-Jupiter-Asteroid gives 91-100). These are numerical conditioning artifacts, not algebraic rank drops. The exact symbolic computation proves the true rank is 116 regardless of mass ratio.
